statusReason: The CLI review round passed, but the recorded monorepo test validator still failed in this workspace and remains documented as a non-green validator result.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Update the `cli-worker` skill to explicitly require a lint run in the completion checklist and align success criteria with scrutiny expectations.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: The `cli-scaffold-and-config` review found `.factory/skills/cli-worker/SKILL.md` requires `cd packages/cli && pnpm lint`, but the reviewed worker transcript/handoff completed without any lint command.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Correct CLI-owned feature metadata so CLI implementation/review tasks use `cli-worker` consistently instead of `backend-worker`.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: The `cli-verify-test-isolation` review found feature metadata marked a CLI-only change as `backend-worker` even though the fix and completed session were fully under `packages/cli`.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Tighten worker handoff guidance to require accurate summaries of whether code was newly implemented versus already present.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: The `cli-issue-commands` review found the handoff claimed the feature was already implemented and tested, but the reviewed commit added substantial new CLI logic and tests.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Refresh CLI manual-validation guidance so it accounts for missions where canonical ports are already occupied by healthy services and workers may need to validate against existing processes instead of launching fresh ones.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: The `cli-comment-commands` review recorded a skill deviation because the skill assumed fresh services on 4200/4201, while the mission state required validating against an existing API and a fallback web port.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Relax the `cli-worker` procedure for narrowly scoped CLI bugfixes so strong automated integration coverage can satisfy verification when it already exercises the changed path, instead of always requiring extra live-server manual runs and a commit step.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Fresh reviews for `fix-cli-identifier-lookup-pagination` and `fix-cli-comment-nonuuid-issue-id-resolution` both found workers deviated from the current `cli-worker` procedure because the existing CLI integration harness already validated the exact changed behaviors, making the mandated extra manual verification and commit flow unnecessarily rigid.
